zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew於1368年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根時，他的父親Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd26歲，母親Crisle verch Gawdyn24歲。他於1406年與Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1419年在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根利安達福享年52歲，埋葬在英國威爾斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew于1368年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根时，他的父亲Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd26岁，母亲Crisle verch Gawdyn24岁。他于1406年与Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1419年在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根利安達福享年52岁，埋葬在英国威尔斯。

nameMeanings.name: Mathew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Mathai, Babu, Oommen, Saji, Varughese, Raju, Reji, Sunil, Prakash, Prasad, Shaji, Cheriyan.
English and Scottish: variant of Matthew . Mathew is common as a personal name among Christians in South India, and was registered as a surname after their immigration to the US.

variants.name: Mathews
meanings: English: patronymic from   Mathew  ; a variant of   Matthews  . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages.

History: Among the earliest bearers of the name in North America was Samuel Mathews (c. 1600–c. 1657), who came to VA from London c. 1618. He established a plantation at the mouth of the Warwick River, which was at first called Mathews Manor; later its name was changed to Denbigh. He was one of the most powerful and influential men in the early affairs of the colony. He (or possibly his son, who bore the same name) was governor of the colony from 1657 until his death in 1660.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Mathew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Ieuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew was born in 1368, in Glamorgan, Wales, his father, Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd, was 26 and his mother, Crisle verch Gawdyn, was 24. He married Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming in 1406, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1419, in Llandaff, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew est né en 1368, à Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Crisle verch Gawdyn, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming en 1406, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1419, à Llandaff, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew 1368 in Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd 26 und seine Mutter Crisle verch Gawdyn 24. Er hat 1406 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1419 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Llandaff, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Wales, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew è nato il nel 1368 nel Glamorgan, Galles, il suo padre, Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Crisle verch Gawdyn, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming nel 1406, nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1419, nel Llandaff, Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathew ap Ieuan Mathewが1368年にウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Sir Ieuan ap Gruffyddは26歳，母Crisle verch Gawdynは24歳であった。1406年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンでJanet Verch Jenkin Flemingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1419年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンランダッフで52歳で死去し，イギリスウェールズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew(이)가 1368년 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Crisle verch Gawdyn(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1406년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1419년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 랜다프에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨일스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew nasceu em 1368, em Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Crisle verch Gawdyn, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming em 1406, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1419, em Llandaff, Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew родился в 1368 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Crisle verch Gawdyn, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming в 1406 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1419 г. в местности лландафф, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathew ap Ieuan Mathew nació en 1368, en Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Sir Ieuan ap Gruffydd, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Crisle verch Gawdyn, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Janet Verch Jenkin Fleming en 1406, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1419, en Llandaff, Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Gales, Reino Unido.
